title: React Cheat Sheet
description: Essential React commands and patterns for engineers

commands.scenario: Use when starting a new React project to set up the basic structure.
Use to start the development server and view your React app in the browser.

commands.scenario: Use at the top of your component files to access React features.

commands.scenario: Use to perform side effects like data fetching in functional components.

commands.scenario: Use to define routes and navigate between different components in a React app.

commands.command: const memoizedValue = useMemo(() => computeExpensiveValue(a, b), [a, b]);
commands.scenario: Use to memoize expensive calculations to improve performance.
